channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: At Chanel, Matthieu Blazy’s Alternative to Ozempic Dressing
item.description: Matthieu Blazy goes beyond the super sucked-in, laced-up styles of the moment. It’s actually not a fairy tale.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Simbarashe Cha/The New York Times

item.title: EU Rejects Plea to Pause Troubled EES Biometrics System at Airports Ahead of Summer Rush
item.description: E.U. leaders rebuffed a plea from the aviation industry to pause the biometric checkpoints, which have caused long lines and missed flights. Summer travelers beware.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung

media:content.media:credit: David W Cerny/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The Entry/Exit System, fully in place since April, requires visitors to the 29 Schengen area countries to provide biometrics like face photos and fingerprints upon entry and exit.

item.title: Fax and Telegram? New York’s Archaic Requirements Get an A.I. Overhaul.
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul will direct state agencies to eliminate or modernize antiquated requirements, and examine if task forces or councils have outlived their purpose.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Cindy Schultz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Kathy Hochul said the Regulatory Reset initiative was designed to make government more efficient and work better for New Yorkers.

item.title: Can America’s Best Engine of Social Mobility Weather a Bad Job Market?
item.description: New York City’s public universities propel young graduates into the middle class. But some worry that a bleaker early career job market has added hurdles.
item.dc:creator: Troy Closson
item.category: Queens College, City University of New York

media:content.media:credit: Camille Farrah Lenain for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graduates of the City University of New York and other colleges are entering the toughest job market since the coronavirus pandemic.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 8, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: A Look Back at Trump’s Broadsides Against NATO Allies
item.description: President Trump has rarely missed an opportunity to castigate the Western military alliance, whose leaders he’s meeting this week, as weak and ineffective.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump has consistently questioned one of NATO’s core provisions: the commitment laid out in Article 5 of its founding charter to treat an attack on one member as an attack on all.

item.title: Live Updates: NATO Leaders Meet at Pivotal Moment for Alliance
item.description: At a summit in Turkey, President Trump is pushing for a smaller role for the United States and for allies to do more for their own defense against an aggressive, militarized Russia.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Hussein Malla/Associated Press

item.title: Late Night Calls a Foul on Trump After His World Cup Intervention
item.description: President Trump may not have known what a red card in soccer is, Ronny Chieng said: “But you know what he did know? How to get a guy a pardon.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Frutos rojos todo el año: lo que hace Driscoll’s
item.description: Este gigante californiano ha contribuido a convertir una delicia local y de temporada en un producto imprescindible en las neveras de todo el mundo y en un fenómeno de mercadotecnia.
item.dc:creator: Julia Moskin

media:content.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times

item.title: We Are Losing the Ability to Discover What We Didn’t Know to Ask
item.description: What used to be a meandering journey is now an immediate arrival at your destination.
item.dc:creator: Anne-Laure Le Cunff

item.title: Oil Prices Jump After U.S. and Iran Trade Strikes in Persian Gulf
item.description: Attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz sparked a new cycle of retaliation that could throttle the flow of energy from the region.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Mohammed Aty/Reuters

item.title: Mideast Live Updates: Iran Launches Attacks Across Gulf After Latest U.S. Strikes
item.description: Iran’s armed forces said they had targeted 85 U.S. military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait. The U.S. said its strikes were in response to what it said were Iranian attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A billboard of the late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, with a note saying “Must rise”, in Tehran last week.

item.title: EE. UU. ataca a Irán y reactiva sanciones por los ataques a buques
item.description: El Comando Central del ejército estadounidense dijo en redes que habían comenzado una serie de ataques. Las autoridades de Irán no han hecho ningún comentario público.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Las negociaciones entre Irán y Estados Unidos se han suspendido hasta que terminen las ceremonias fúnebres de varios días en Teherán por el ayatolá Alí Jameneí, el líder supremo que murió el primer día de la guerra.

item.title: Germans Don’t Love Budweiser. It Won’t Take No for an Answer.
item.description: Not for the first time, Budweiser is trying to make it in Germany. But this is a tough time to sell German drinkers on the image and taste of American beer.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley

media:content.media:credit: Jim Tankersley/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A box of Anheuser-Busch Bud beer at a supermarket in Berlin.

item.title: Una llamada de Trump a la FIFA convierte el Mundial en otro frente político
item.description: La acción del mandatario de EE. UU. deja claro que todavía no ha encontrado ningún ámbito de la vida estadounidense, ni siquiera mundial, en el que no esté dispuesto a imponer su voluntad.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente Donald Trump recibiendo el recién creado “Premio de la Paz de la FIFA” de manos de Gianni Infantino, presidente de la FIFA, el año pasado. Trump llamó a Infantino para protestar por la suspensión de un jugador estadounidense.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Loss for U.S. Soccer, but Leap by Soccer in U.S.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for July 8, 2026.

item.title: Trump’s Arrival in Turkey for NATO Summit Adds Drama and Spectacle
item.description: The second President Trump landed in Turkey, the center of gravity shifted right to where he likes it best: himself.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh and Tyler Pager

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump with President Recep Tayyip Erdogan of Turkey after arriving in Ankara on Tuesday.

item.title: What to Know About Australia’s Contest With China in the Pacific After Beijing Tested a Missile
item.description: With a series of recent treaties and alliances, Canberra is seeking to prevent Beijing from establishing a military foothold in the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim

media:content.media:credit: Leon Lord/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese of Australia, left, and his counterpart from Fiji, Sitiveni Rabuka, signed two security agreements, in Suva, Fiji, on Monday.

item.title: Manhattan Building’s Columns Buckled Beneath New Addition, Images Show
item.description: Images and videos of the Manhattan building analyzed by The Times show that the structural columns buckled directly beneath where the recent addition was built.
item.dc:creator: James Glanz, Simon Ducroquet, Helmuth Rosales and Charlie Smart

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Charges Against Man Who Drove Family Off Cliff Are Dropped After Treatment
item.description: Three counts of attempted murder against Dharmesh A. Patel were dismissed after he completed a court-ordered mental health program, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Mack Liederman

item.title: Haley Stevens Goes on Attack Against Abdul El-Sayed at Michigan Senate Debate
item.description: At their first one-on-one debate in a top Senate race, Representative Haley Stevens accused her progressive rival, Dr. Abdul El-Sayed, of being too focused on publicity as he called her a tool of corporate interests.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Esler/WOOD TV8
media:content.media:description: Dr. Abdul El-Sayed and Representative Haley Stevens met on Tuesday night in Grand Rapids for their first Democratic primary debate since a third candidate dropped out.

item.title: Dozens Face Felony Charges After Raid of Beagle Research Facility
item.description: The charges for most of the people carry sentences of up to 12.5 years in prison, officials said, and the police have recommended more charges for a separate attempt in April.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:credit: Taylor Glascock for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Activists were enveloped in tear gas during an attempt to raid Ridglan Farms to rescue beagles in Blue Mounds, Wis., in April.

item.title: New York City Adopts Measures to Slow Legionnaires’ Disease Outbreak
item.description: An ongoing cluster of the illness has sickened 23 people. That’s not unusual: Most years, 200 to 700 people in the city are diagnosed with the disease.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Goldstein

media:content.media:credit: Mimi d’Autremont for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tests performed in a New York City Department of Health lab identify the Legionella bacterium.

item.title: Top Maine Democrat Says Platner Will Have ‘No Role’ in Picking Next Nominee
item.description: A top state party official said that Graham Platner’s team had “repeatedly” tried to “put their thumb on the scale,” but he would not influence how the party chose a new Senate candidate.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner has faced widespread calls from top Democrats to end his Senate campaign after a woman accused him of rape.

item.title: Midtown Building Developer Says Problems Were a ‘Typical Construction Mishap’
item.description: Nathan Berman, one of the developers of the Midtown building that suffered structural damage, predicted the project would restart within weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The developer of the East 42nd Street building that suffered structural damage predicted the project would start up again soon.

item.title: Judge Orders D.H.S. to Restore 4 States’ Access to Citizenship Data
item.description: The ruling, based on an agreement the Trump administration signed with Florida last year, contradicted an earlier order by a judge in Washington that required the access be suspended.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague and David Ovalle

media:content.media:credit: Zack Wittman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Election workers counting ballots in Largo, Fla., in 2024. An order by a federal judge in Florida on Tuesday allowed bulk searches of a federal citizenship database by state officials to check voter eligibility.

item.title: Part of Florida’s ‘Stop WOKE’ Law Ruled Illegal by Appeals Court
item.description: The law limits teaching on race, gender and other topics. Part of the law related to colleges was struck down. It remains in effect in K-12 schools.
item.dc:creator: Vimal Patel

media:content.media:credit: Chris O'Meara/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Gov. Ron DeSantis of Florida signed the “Stop WOKE” Act to limit teaching about race, gender and other topics.

item.title: Suspect in Charlie Kirk Shooting Talked to Turning Point USA Members, Agent Testifies
item.description: Prosecutors questioned the lead investigator about Tyler Robinson’s interactions with members of Charlie Kirk’s Turning Point USA during the second day of a preliminary hearing.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:credit: Spenser Heaps/Associated Press

item.title: Midtown Neighborhood Has to Cope in a Troubled Building’s Shadow
item.description: Travelers were shut out of major hotels, and work ground to a halt for some on Tuesday after a building partially buckled in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Freeman

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: East 44th Street in Manhattan on Tuesday, as evacuations continued after a high-rise office building on 42nd Street was at risk of partial collapse.

item.title: India Calls Out Meta Over Reported Child Sexual Abuse Imagery in Ads
item.description: The tech giant that owns Instagram, Facebook and WhatsApp says it is in touch with the Indian authorities about reports that exploitative content is being hawked on one of its platforms.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin

media:content.media:credit: Francis Mascarenhas/Reuters
media:content.media:description: A Meta logo at a conference in Mumbai, India.

item.title: Why Israelis and Palestinians Need New Narratives
item.description: A Land for All is a joint Israeli-Palestinian initiative advocating options beyond the two-state or one-state solution — namely, a confederacy model in which Israel and Palestine would exist as separate political entities but with free movement across borders. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the executive directors May Pundak and Rula Hardal explain their long-term vision for the region, and why both Israelis and Palestinians need new national narratives.

item.title: As Israel Loses Support in the U.S., Rahm Emanuel Criticizes Netanyahu
item.description: The former Chicago mayor, a Democrat exploring a 2028 presidential run, is in Tel Aviv calling for an end to unconditional U.S. support of Israel.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Rahm Emanuel, the former Chicago mayor and White House chief of staff, is trying to chart a course between the anti-Israel left and the pro-Israel right.

item.title: ‘We Palestinians Are Not Going to Give Up’
item.description: The Palestinian advocate Rula Hardal is a co-executive director of A Land for All, a joint Israeli-Palestinian initiative to imagine a new future for the region. She shares her motivation to find new solutions on “The Ezra Klein Show.”

item.title: ‘This Has Not Given Us Safety’
item.description: The Israeli activist May Pundak advocated the two-state solution for many years, but her confidence faded as she saw the idea lose relevance. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” Pundak explains why there’s a need for a new way of thinking — and a new plan for the future of the region.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran and Reimposes Sanctions After Tanker Attacks
item.description: The military operation came hours after the U.S. Treasury revoked a waiver allowing global sales of Iranian oil.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Negotiations between Iran and the United States have been paused until after multiday funeral ceremonies in Tehran for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the supreme leader who was killed on the first day of the war.

item.title: These Maine Democrats Might Replace Graham Platner in the Senate Race
item.description: Mr. Platner has not yet withdrawn from the race after a rape allegation, but Democrats in the state and nationally are already exploring potential alternatives.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Cj Gunther/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A wide range of Democrats, including many former allies, have called on Graham Platner to end his campaign for Senate in Maine.

item.title: What to Know About Manhattan Office Conversions
item.description: The city has encouraged more complex office-to-residential conversions to fight the housing crisis.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth A. Harris and Aimee Ortiz

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Several streets in Midtown were blocked off on Tuesday as officials feared for a partial collapse of 235 East 42nd St, center.

item.title: Rush to Evacuate as ‘Beams Started Bending’ and Floors Sagged at Midtown Tower
item.description: Offices and hotels around East 42nd Street were evacuated after structural damage affected an office conversion project, disrupting businesses, stranding tourists and snarling traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Southall and Mihir Zaveri

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The partial collapse of a 37-story office building being converted into housing on East 42nd Street disrupted Midtown Manhattan on Tuesday.

item.title: Maryland Moves Toward Redistricting Ahead of 2028
item.description: Lawmakers at a special legislative session next month will try to pass an amendment to the state constitution that should ensure new congressional maps survive a legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson

media:content.media:credit: Robert Yoon/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The Maryland State House in Annapolis.

item.title: Sagging Manhattan Building Stable for Now, Official Says
item.description: A high-rise Midtown Manhattan office building being converted to apartments showed signs of collapsing on Tuesday. A city official later said the building was stable for now.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

item.title: Judge Quashes Justice Dept. Subpoena in 2020 Election Inquiry
item.description: The ruling cast serious doubts on the department’s efforts to reinvestigate the race, finding that it was too late to bring criminal charges in the case.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer

media:content.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Members of the F.B.I. outside of the Fulton County elections office in Georgia this year.

item.title: Democrats Clash Over a Replacement for Platner
item.description: Also, U.S. forces carry out strikes in Iran. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times

item.title: Zimbabwe’s President Signs Law Extending His Term
item.description: The measure will allow the president, Emmerson Mnangagwa, to stay in office until 2030. Critics say the move tightens his autocratic grip on power and erodes the nation’s democracy.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Emmerson Mnangagwa of Zimbabwe at the United Nations headquarters in New York City in 2022.

item.title: Utah Moves to Close Teen Treatment Center Accused of Abusing Girls
item.description: Paris Hilton helped lead a campaign to shut down the Provo Canyon School, where she said she was beaten and spat on when she was 17.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Schoenbaum/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Paris Hilton, the celebrity socialite, outside a Provo Canyon School building in Springville, Utah, last month. She has described being beaten at the school when she was a teenager.

item.title: ICE Officer Kills a Mexican Man in Houston
item.description: The agency said the man was an “illegal alien” who had tried to run over the agent, though no evidence was immediately offered.
item.dc:creator: Edgar Sandoval

media:content.media:credit: Jacob Lujan/Houston Chronicle, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The Houston Fire Department said the man was shot in the abdomen.

item.title: Penelope Keith, Effortlessly Posh Star of British Sitcoms, Dies at 86
item.description: A veteran stage actress, she starred in two of Britain’s most beloved TV comedies of the 1970s, “The Good Life” and “To the Manor Born.”
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

media:content.media:credit: Don Smith/Radio Times, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Penelope Keith in 1977 in a scene from the sitcom “The Good Life.”

item.title: Las misteriosas esferas encontradas en Australia probablemente sean basura espacial
item.description: El hallazgo desconcertó —y después divirtió— a los vecinos de un pueblo del noreste de Australia. La agencia espacial del país dijo que los objetos parecen ser restos de un lanzamiento espacial.
item.dc:creator: Hari Raj

media:content.media:credit: Bethlehem Feleke/The New York Times

item.title: Midtown Evacuations Scramble Plans for Hotel Guests
item.description: At least two hotels near the unstable building were evacuated, stranding travelers and forcing guests to clamber for alternative accommodation.
item.dc:creator: Ryley Ober

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Sansin Tokyay, right, rolling her luggage up Third Avenue. She was forced to evacuate the Westin New York Grand Central on East 42nd Street.
